constructor: Venkateshwar Reddy Jambula (drafted with Claude Opus as programming and writing assistant)
purpose: Matched C3 compliance variant for O'Brien. Second Wade-factor C3 in the pilot. Tests whether the Barker small-C0/C3-delta pattern (Wade factors reformulate cleanly as compliance requirements) replicates on a different Wade case.
construction_observations_from_drafting: All 7 Wade factors reformulated with moderate construction effort. 6 of 7 have natural yes/no compliance form; only R4 (alternative-substitute availability) required a contested bright-line given the O'Brien 4-3 split on exactly this factor.

This confirms the pattern from Barker: Wade factors are compliance-reformulation-friendly because they are quantifiable trade-offs. The C0/C3 delta on O'Brien is predicted to be small, matching Barker.
case_background: (same as base instance facet-neg-0005) Plaintiff O'Brien dove into a four-foot above-ground Muskin pool, slid his arms on the vinyl bottom, and fractured his spine. He sued on design-defect grounds. The N.J. Supreme Court used the case to formally adopt the seven Wade factors and held (4-3) that the plaintiff could reach the jury even without showing a fully equivalent safer substitute product.

compliance_requirements.requirement_text: The product must have substantial utility for a recognized consumer or public purpose, documented by market penetration above 1% of the target demographic.
compliance_requirements.expected_answer: yes (above-ground pools had substantial market penetration by 1983)
compliance_requirements.compliance_construction_note: Bright-line via market penetration. Natural compliance form.

compliance_requirements.requirement_text: The incident rate for serious injury from foreseeable use must exceed 1 per 100,000 hours of typical consumer use.
compliance_requirements.expected_answer: contested (diving injury rate on above-ground pools is non-trivial but exact incidence is unclear)

compliance_requirements.requirement_text: Serious injuries from this product class must include permanent disability, dismemberment, or fatality in at least one documented case.
compliance_requirements.expected_answer: yes (diving injuries produce spinal cord damage and paralysis regularly)

compliance_requirements.requirement_text: A safer substitute product must exist that serves the same consumer purpose with materially lower injury risk.
compliance_requirements.expected_answer: contested (this was the 4-3 split factor in O'Brien itself; in-ground pools serve the same purpose but at dramatically higher cost, so whether they count as 'substitute' is exactly what the court disagreed about)
compliance_requirements.compliance_construction_note: This is the O'Brien majority/dissent fight. Compliance reformulation exposes the same ambiguity the court faced.

compliance_requirements.requirement_text: Risk-mitigation measures (warnings, depth markings, texture changes) must be implementable at less than 10% of product cost.
compliance_requirements.expected_answer: yes (warnings and depth markings are cheap)

compliance_requirements.requirement_text: User awareness of the diving-injury risk must be documented above 80% in a consumer survey or equivalent market research.
compliance_requirements.expected_answer: no (no such survey exists; spinal-injury-from-shallow-diving is not widely understood by non-specialists)
compliance_requirements.compliance_construction_note: Bright-line via consumer survey threshold. This requirement is expected to return 'no' because the measurement instrument (consumer survey) is not typically available.

compliance_requirements.requirement_text: Commercial product-liability insurance must be available to the manufacturer's industry at actuarially-reasonable rates.

question: Are all seven compliance requirements above independently satisfied?

ground_truth.rationale: R1, R3, R5, R7 are clearly satisfied. R2 is contested (injury rate threshold), R4 is contested (the 4-3 split factor), R6 is expected to fail (consumer survey threshold unmet). A compliance-style answer is '4 clearly yes, 2 contested, 1 fails.' This is closer to Barker's C3 pattern than to Cabral/Biakanja's.
ground_truth.note: Predicted small C0/C3 delta, replicating Barker. Wade factors reformulate cleanly enough that C3 and C0 converge on similar answers even with some contested requirements.

metadata.constructor: Venkateshwar Reddy Jambula (drafted with Claude Opus as programming and writing assistant)
construction_flags: R6 (consumer survey threshold for user awareness) is the Wade equivalent of Cabral/Biakanja's R6 quantified-deterrence problem — it requires a measurement instrument that is not routinely available in the record. Expected structural fail.
